title: Pigs, protests and police: How tensions flared over Bakrid goats in Mira Road housing society
content: Tensions flared in Mira Road after a video of a policeman with a pig went viral amid a dispute over goats kept in a housing society for Bakrid. The municipal corporation removed the goats, and police maintained a heavy presence. Local leaders called for animal sacrifices only at designated sites, with some Hindu groups protesting with pigs.

title: Byju's crisis explained: What went wrong & how India’s edtech giant spiralled into legal turmoil
content: Edtech giant Byju's founder Byju Raveendran received a six-month prison sentence in Singapore for contempt of court over asset disclosure non-compliance. This latest setback adds to the company's mounting legal woes, including fund diversion allegations and creditor claims, marking a significant downfall for the once-celebrated startup.

title: Flights affected: Air India reduces domestic frequency by 22% amid rising fuel prices
content: Air India has temporarily reduced operations on select domestic routes between June and August 2026 due to high fuel prices and ongoing West Asia conflict impacts. This follows similar adjustments to international services, with the airline citing sustained operational pressures and a need for stability amid global aviation uncertainty.

title: Randhir Singh, India’s first Asiad shooting gold medallist, passes away at 79
content: Randhir Singh, India’s first Asian Games shooting gold medallist and a veteran sports administrator, passed away at 79 after battling age-related illnesses. A five-time Olympian, he later served as Secretary General of the Indian Olympic Association and President of the Olympic Council of Asia, leaving behind a lasting legacy in Indian and Asian sports.

title: 'SIR breathes life into Constitution': SC upholds EC's roll revision exercise — top quotes
content: Delivering its verdict on a batch of pleas questioning the legality of the SIR exercise, the apex court identified three key questions for consideration. These included whether the ECI possessed the authority to conduct the revision, whether the inquiry pursued a legitimate objective while meeting the test of proportionality, and whether the procedure adopted was consistent with the statutory framework governing electoral rolls.

title: Cloudflare lays off 1,100 employees; CEO says company isn’t struggling
content: Cloudflare is laying off over 1,100 employees, about 20% of its workforce, to restructure around AI adoption. CEO Matthew Prince explained the cuts target 'measurers' – roles like middle management and operations – as AI enhances efficiency. He believes AI will boost productivity for 'builders' and 'sellers', allowing greater investment in growth areas.

title: Siddaramaiah reacts amid speculations over Karnataka CM post; BJP invokes OBC angle
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah offered a guarded response to leadership change speculation, stating he would speak tomorrow.  Reports suggest a national role for him, paving the way for Deputy CM DK Shivakumar.  The BJP criticized the Congress over the power struggle, warning of electoral consequences and questioning governance improvements.

title: 'Stay hydrated': PM Modi lists precautions amid heatwave, urges to not forget animals
content: In light of the escalating heatwave sweeping across India, Prime Minister Narendra Modi is calling on citizens to prioritize their health. He stresses the importance of staying thoroughly hydrated, keeping water on hand at all times, and being alert to signs of heat stress, such as dizziness and fatigue.

title: 'False, one-sided narrative': Byju Raveendran reacts to Singapore court order
content: Byju Raveendran, the founder of failed edtech firm Byju’s, has been handed a six-month jail sentence by a Singapore court in a contempt case, as per a Bloomberg report.

title: Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: 'Not in Test cricket': Ganguly on what Sooryavanshi needs to do next
content: Sourav Ganguly believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is India's future but not yet ready for Test cricket, advocating for his inclusion in T20s. He also feels Auqib Nabi deserved a spot in the India squad alongside Prince Yadav. Ganguly acknowledges Rishabh Pant's Test prowess while noting his T20 struggles and the challenges of captaincy.

title: 'EC didn’t act outside its statutory powers': SC upholds SIR exercise in Bihar, Bengal & other states
content: ​​A Bench led by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi pronounced the verdict on Wednesday after reserving judgment this year in January following extensive hearings in the matter. The petitions had challenged the legality of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise undertaken by the ECI, arguing that the process went beyond the powers granted to the poll body under Article 326 of the Constitution, the Representation of the People Act, 1950, and the rules framed under it.

title: Uber COO says Claude Code, ChatGPT increased productivity by 25%
content: Uber's COO reveals AI coding tools boost internal productivity by 25%, but a direct link to better consumer features remains elusive.  Despite cost concerns and slower hiring, the company sees AI as an accelerator, empowering employees and significantly increasing code commits per engineer, though human oversight is crucial.

title: Ranveer Singh film producers may approach CCI over FWICE’s ‘Don 3’ non-cooperation directive
content: Ranveer Singh faces a production halt as the FWICE issued a non-cooperation directive after his 'Don 3' exit. This impacts upcoming films like 'Pralay' and 'Chandragupta Maurya', potentially affecting hundreds of daily wage workers. Producers are considering legal action, citing anti-competitive practices, while FWICE insists the directive is effective. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Watch: Kohli pulls off Ronaldo’s 'SIUUU' celebration after GT collapse
content: Cricket star Virat Kohli, a known Cristiano Ronaldo fan, celebrated a crucial wicket with the Portuguese footballer's iconic 'SIUUU' during RCB's Qualifier 1 win. This isn't the first time Kohli has paid tribute to his idol on the field, having previously performed the celebration in other major tournaments.

title: Red Balloon launches India’s 1st super pressure balloon, eyes entry into elite near-space club
content: India's first indigenous super pressure balloon, developed by Red Balloon Aerospace, has successfully launched from Vijayawada. This near-space technology aims to set a new national endurance record of up to 24 hours in the stratosphere, carrying payloads for monitoring and telecommunications. The innovation promises affordable stratospheric access, potentially transforming rural connectivity and disaster response.

title: Bakrid tensions flare near Mumbai as goats removed from Mira Road society amid protests
content: Tensions erupted in Mira Road over goats kept in a housing society ahead of Bakri Eid. Following viral videos and an injury, authorities removed around 40-50 goats. Local residents and politicians emphasized the need for designated sacrifice sites, with a former MP calling for a ban on society premises. Leaders urged peaceful celebrations.

title: SRH vs RR, IPL 2026 Eliminator: How new-ball bowlers can become the real trump card in the contest of power-hitters
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad and Rajasthan Royals face off in a high-stakes IPL 2026 Eliminator in Mullanpur, where explosive batting is expected again. SRH’s powerful top order, led by Abhishek Sharma and Heinrich Klaasen, takes on RR’s young star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Jofra Archer. Despite the season’s batting dominance, the match could hinge on which team’s new-ball bowlers strike early and expose vulnerable middle orders.

title: Watch: Heatwave sparks massive fire in Himachal forests; IAF helicopters deployed
content: Indian Air Force helicopters are battling a major forest fire in Himachal Pradesh's Kasauli. Mi-17 and Chinook choppers are dousing flames in inaccessible areas, even ferrying water from Sukhna Lake. Thick smoke billowed from the scenic town's forests, including orchards and oak trees, as authorities continue efforts to control the blaze.

title: Another abuse scandal rocks France after Gisèle Pelicot, this time involving children
content: In a troubling turn of events, Paris finds itself at the center of a child abuse controversy, with reports surpassing 100 allegations of mistreatment and sexual assault within schools. Fueled by the Gisèle Pelicot case, concerned parents are pushing for thorough investigations and accountability from authorities.

title: 'That's a terrible question': Phillips loses cool during presser after defeat
content: Glenn Phillips reacted angrily after Gujarat Titans’ 92-run loss to RCB in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. When a reporter asked if GT had mentally given up while chasing 255, Phillips called it a “silly” and “terrible” question, insisting professional cricketers never quit. He admitted scoreboard pressure hurt GT after early wickets, but said the team must now stay calm and prepare for the Eliminator.

title: Byju’s founder sentenced to 6 months in jail for contempt of court; ordered to pay $70,500 in costs
content: The ruling marks another setback for the entrepreneur, who is already facing legal challenges from overseas investors across multiple jurisdictions. In the United States, lenders are attempting to recover losses tied to a soured $1.2 billion loan.

title: Woman arriving from Uganda tests negative for Ebola, govt on alert - what we know so far
content: A traveler returning from Uganda has been isolated in Bengaluru with mild body ache, prompting heightened surveillance by Indian authorities. While no confirmed Ebola case has been detected in India, the individual is undergoing testing as a precautionary measure amid ongoing outbreaks in Africa.

title: India’s UFC trailblazer Puja Tomar walks into the biggest fight of her career yet
content: Pooja Tomar stands at a career crossroads as she prepares to face China’s Shi Ming in her third UFC bout in Macau. At 32, victory is pivotal for India’s first female UFC fighter to keep her momentum and future options alive. Yet, beyond results, Tomar’s impact on Indian MMA has already been significant, with the Budhana-born fighter helping bring unprecedented visibility to the sport while continuing to dream of an eventual IPL-like moment for MMA in India.

title: Indian Navy foils possible piracy attack near merchant vessel in Western Indian Ocean
content: INS Kolkata thwarted a potential pirate attack on merchant vessel MV Mashallah 1 in the Western Indian Ocean. The Indian Navy warship, on anti-piracy patrol, swiftly deployed its helicopter for surveillance and conducted boarding operations, ensuring the safety of the ship and its crew. This intervention highlights the Navy's commitment to protecting vital shipping lanes.

title: He kept going when every result said no: Zomato rider who made it to FMS Delhi
content: Girish Sharma's journey from Zomato delivery rider to Faculty of Management Studies, Delhi, highlights relentless perseverance. Facing multiple rejections and waitlists after two failed CAT attempts, Sharma refused to settle for less. His story emphasises unwavering determination and the refusal to accept limitations, proving that sustained effort, not just inspiration, drives success.

title: The urge to purge: In Trump’s GOP, disloyalty is political death
content: Republican politicians face a stark choice: unwavering loyalty to Donald Trump or political oblivion. Recent primary defeats, like Senator John Cornyn's, underscore Trump's absolute control.  Conservatism alone is insufficient; submission to Trump is paramount.  This dynamic forces GOP figures to prioritize his approval, even as national polls show unease, highlighting a deep ideological divide within the party.

title: Delhi govt monitors transportation, sale and sacrifice of animals ahead of Eid
content: Delhi authorities have deployed rapid response teams across all districts to prevent illegal animal sacrifice and cruelty ahead of Bakrid.  Strict guidelines prohibit public slaughter, sale of animals in unauthorized spots, and the sacrifice of specific animals.  Criminal cases will be filed for violations, with awareness campaigns also planned.

title: ‘If you can’t give us roads, give us choppers’: Chhattisgarh villagers to Amit Shah
content: Residents of a secluded Chhattisgarh village, feeling overlooked after a decade of an unfinished road project, have taken a bold step in their appeal to the Union home minister: if roads are not feasible, then they request a helicopter to handle urgent situations.

title: Rajat Patidar walks the talk with a six-hitting masterclass in Dharamsala
content: Rajat Patidar backed his pre-match claim of preferring pace over spin with a breathtaking 93 off 33 balls against Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. The RCB skipper smashed nine sixes -- seven against pacers -- and now has 41 sixes this IPL season, with 27 coming against fast bowlers. Batting at No. 4, Patidar has scored 486 runs in 14 matches at a strike rate of 196.76, underlining his impact as one of RCB's most destructive middle-order batters.

title: '‘I'm a big fan of dreams...': Quote of the day by Kevin Costner
content: Veteran actor Kevin Costner believes dreams are often the first casualty as people embrace societal norms and 'reality.'  He shared this sentiment, explaining that many abandon their passions for conventional paths. Costner himself chose to pursue acting despite a stable career, highlighting the personal sacrifice involved in chasing aspirations.

title: Harmful mirchi tadka: 802kg fake spices found in Old Delhi masala hub
content: Authorities in Delhi have busted an illegal spice manufacturing unit in Khari Baoli, seizing over 800 kg of counterfeit spices. The operation uncovered grinding machinery and a large quantity of non-edible adulterants, including industrial dyes, mixed with common spices like red chilli and turmeric.

title: Norway Chess: Divya beats India No. 1 Humpy; no joy for Gukesh, Pragg
content: Youngster Divya Deshmukh continued her fine start at Norway Chess by defeating country No. 1 Koneru Humpy in an Armageddon tiebreak after a tactical classical draw. It was a bleak day for India in the open section, however, as R Praggnanandhaa lost in classical to Alireza Firouzja, while D Gukesh fell to Wesley So in Armageddon.

title: Anushka spotted wearing ‘Radha Naam Jap’ counter during Kohli’s match; fans react
content: Anushka Sharma passionately cheered for husband Virat Kohli and Royal Challengers Bengaluru during their IPL match. Captured praying and chanting, her devotion resonated with fans.  A heartwarming moment also saw her respond with a smile and wave to a fan's greeting, showcasing her engaging personality amidst the high-stakes game.

title: Bank holiday today: Are banks open or closed for Bakri Eid? Check state-wise list
content: Banks across numerous Indian states are closed today for Eid-ul-Adha, impacting branch services like cheque clearance and cash deposits. Customers are urged to verify local holiday schedules. While physical banking is halted, digital services including internet banking, mobile apps, and ATMs remain fully operational. Plan ahead for essential branch tasks and utilize online channels to avoid disruption.

title: Arun Dhumal Exclusive: Sooryavanshi embodies IPL's tagline of 'where talent meets opportunity'
content: In an IPL season bereft of the talent and regular presence of MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma, 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has seemingly taken over the mantle. As the teenager dazzles with explosive batting, IPL Chairman Arun Dhumal says the BCCI and Rajasthan Royals must help nurture his rise and prepare him for the challenges ahead.

title: India rips into Pakistan at UN, flags 'bleeding India by a thousand cuts' doctrine
content: India strongly criticized Pakistan at the UN Security Council, accusing it of sponsoring cross-border terrorism and violating sovereignty through decades of aggression. India's envoy stated Pakistan's actions, including its "doctrine of bleeding India by a thousand cuts," expose its hollow rhetoric on the UN Charter.

title: Govt panel to examine 'unnatural demographic changes' due to illegal immigration
content: A high-level committee, headed by retired Supreme Court judge Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar, has been formed to examine demographic changes in India, including those caused by illegal immigration and abnormal population shifts. The committee will assess causes and impacts, recommending policy and legal frameworks to address these challenges within a year.

title: Andhra Pradesh cannot afford YSRCP’s return in 2029, says Chandrababu Naidu at Mahanadu
content: Addressing the inaugural session of Mahanadu 2026, the Chief Minister calls for adherence to ‘coalition dharma’ and says the present government is still undoing the damage of the 2019-24 YSRCP rule

title: CPI(M) cadres stage protests after ED raids ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s residence in Kannur
content: The CPI(M) leadership strongly condemned the searches, challenging that the BJP-led union government was using Central agencies to target opposition leaders through politically motivated actions

title: Supreme Court sets aside NCLAT order which rejected Amazon's appeal
content: Supreme Court overturns NCLAT ruling, allowing Amazon’s appeal against anti-trust suspension of its Future Group investment deal

title: Uganda national in Bengaluru tests negative for Ebola; no confirmed case in India: Health Ministry
content: A 28-year-old woman from Uganda, who developed mild symptoms including body ache, was shifted from a hotel to the State-run Epidemic Diseases Hospital on May 26

title: Leadership change in Karnataka: All eyes on CM Siddaramaiah’s breakfast meeting with Cabinet colleagues on May 28
content: On the list of invitees are his Cabinet colleagues, including his deputy D.K. Shivakumar, who has been a contender for the top post ever since the Congress government reached the halfway mark of its five-year-tenure in November 2025

title: T.N. government to rope in external agency for used liquor bottle collection, says Prohibition Minister
content: It would be implemented by the end of June, completely doing away with the present practice of employees handling used empty liquor bottles, the Minister said

title: Supreme Court upholds SIR exercise as an advancement towards free and fair elections
content: The court upholds the Election Commission’s power to check citizenship for limited purpose of verifying inclusion in electoral roll

title: Lokesh proposes 33% of TDP seats for women in 2029 elections at Mahanadu
content: The TDP national working president says the party will give women a 33% quota whether or not the Women’s Reservation Bill is passed, and sets out the proposal at Mahanadu near Mangalagiri

title: Veteran sports administrator Randhir Singh passes away
content: Veteran sports administrator Randhir Singh, India’s first shooting gold-medallist, has passed away at 79 in New Delhi

title: Handloom, handicraft artisans earn about ₹7,000 as monthly wages, finds study
content: The survey, covering Tamil Nadu and six other States, reflects the workforce’s financial vulnerability and raises critical concerns about future patterns of fair earnings and fair trade

title: Tourists dash to Shimla as plains sizzle; 70,000 vehicles reach city in 72 hours
content: Normally, the peak tourist season starts in Himachal Pradesh in the first week of June, which has been advanced this year due to heatwave-like conditions sweeping across India

title: ED raids on Pinarayi Vijayan: CPI(M) says will resist politically and legally; party workers protest across Kerala
content: CPI(M) State Secretary M.V. Govindan says raids are ‘the latest instance of a planned attack’ against CPI(M) by Sangh Parivar and RSS

title: Navy foils piracy attempt, secures merchant vessel in western Indian Ocean
content: During the mission, INS Kolkata also ensured safe passage for the merchant vessel MV Mashaallah 1 through the sensitive maritime zone

title: A journey of the heart: One Sri Lankan officer’s transformative train adventure through India
content: Forty days, some 13,000 kilometres, km, and 24 trains crisscrossing India — that sums up Saman Athaudahetti’s February and March, as he chased his long-held dream of exploring this neighbouring country through its railway network

title: 9,438 snakebite cases in Jharkhand since 2022, health department asks doctors to comply with guidelines
content: The National Action Plan launched by the National Centre for Disease Control aims to reduce snakebite deaths and disabilities by 50% by the year 2030

title: Sulur girl sexual assault and murder case: Goondas Act invoked against two accused
content: Coimbatore District Collector Pavankumar G. Giriyappanavar invoked the Goondas Act against the two accused after categorising them as sexual offenders

title: FIR against Mamata Banerjee for remarks ‘hurting religious sentiments’
content: FIR filed against former West Bengal Chief Minsiter Mamata Banerjee for remarks allegedly hurting religious sentiments, prompting police investigation into the matter

title: Bihar Govt. employees mandated to visit tourist spots every three months and file reports
content: The State Government has asked employees to travel with their families to tourist destinations within Bihar and submit personal experiences, details of the destinations visited and photographs

title: Ahmedabad Air India crash: Gujarat government to rebuild hostel damaged in AI-171 crash; victims' kin seek memorial at site
content: The government will build a new ultra-modern hostel facility at the same site at an estimated cost of ₹ 105 crore, says Health Minister Praful Pansheriya

title: Arya Vysya leaders seek action against YouTuber over remarks on T.G. Venkatesh
content: Former Rajya Sabha member T.G. Venkatesh and the Arya Vysya Mahasabha demand a criminal case against analyst ‘Punch’ Prabhakar and say they will hold district-wide protests

title: Rajasthan High Court acquits Asaram of gang rape charges, upholds life term in minor rape case
content: The court directed Asaram to surrender in view of the conviction; he is currently out on temporary bail, which was extended by seven days on May 25

title: Assam filmmaker’s documentary screened at British Film Institute in London
content: Chalo India with Ericji (Varanasi) was showcased at the BFI after its selection in the Indian Panorama Non-Feature section at the 56th International Film Festival of India

title: Bose Krishnamachari quit Kochi Biennale following sexual harassment allegation, says foundation
content: KBF chairperson V. Venu says the complaint triggered an internal investigation, though no police report has been filed

title: Natarajasundaram Pillai, the nagaswaram player who excelled in complex rhythms
content:
Pallavi Natarajasundaram Pillai, as he was known, was renowned for mallaris. The available recordings are an invaluable documentation of his style and a testimony to his astonishing grip on layam or thalam. There would be no dull moment in a concert by Natarajasundaram Pillai, says thavil  maestro 
Haridwaramangalam A.K. Palanivel

title: SC upholds SIR, says it is in consonance with Representation of the People Act: highlights
content: The Election Commission’s decision to conduct the SIR remains within statutory mandate, the Supreme Court said

title: Kochi-Muziris Biennale: the brightest jewel in the city’s crown
content: The country’s only art biennale of international clout shows the world what art is to the city, or rather what the city is to art. It is also the city’s biggest achievement when it comes to art and its showcase

title: New documentary on late Bhupen Hazarika premiered
content: The documentary titled Bhupen da Uncut premieres ahead of birth centenary celebrations, featuring vintage footage and “unpublished” poems

title: Assamese film Goodbye Guruji selected for Paris festival
content: Writer-director Arunjit Borah’s movie is a story of transformation through education and perseverance

title: What to expect from the 63rd edition of the Bengaluru Ganesh Utsava in 2025
content: The 11-day festival will have musical performances by Vijay Prakash, Raghu Dixit, Rajesh Krishnan, Vijay Yesudas, M.D. Pallavi, and Praveen Godkhindi, as well as along with a food festival and cultural events

title: Njattyela Sreedharan, self-taught linguist and creator of four-language Dravidian dictionary, passes away at 87
content: Sreedharan was celebrated for compiling Chathur Dravida Bhasha Padhaparichayam, a work hailed as a milestone in the study of South Indian languages

title: Third edition of AIAC hosts 21 artists from nine ASEAN countries
content: Over 10 days, artists will create original works, take part in workshops on classical dance, music, and handicrafts, and explore India’s artistic traditions in a collaborative setting

title: ABC 6.0: Building a sense of camaraderie through art
content: The coming week will see the Art Bengaluru Collective will hold the sixth edition of their multi-art show this weekend

title: Ragbag 2025 in Thiruvananthapuram, to feature curated performances, food and craft
content: The international performing arts festival has acts from European and Latin American countries, a food festival curated by Anumitra Ghosh Dastidar and a craft bazaar curated by Jaya Jaitly

title: Ustad Zakir Hussain held Kerala’s traditional percussion culture in high esteem, says veteran percussionist Mattannur Sankarankutty Marar
content: Mattannur Sankarankutty Marar reminisces about his deep bond with tabla maestro Zakir Hussain, highlighting their fusion performances and personal connection

title: Ninasam Tirugata stages two plays during annual ‘Conversation with the Arts’ event at Heggodu
content: The trained artists of Ninasam Rangashikshana Kendra performed the plays and won the hearts of the audience

title: Visakhapatnam to host World Food India South edition on July 9, 10
content: The southern regional summit at Andhra University Engineering College Grounds will bring together food processing, exports and investment stakeholders from eight southern States and Union Territories

title: Hyderabad’s QT centre: A safe haven for the queer and trans community
content: The initiative, which opened its doors in April 2023, functions as a an all-round resource centre offering mental health support, legal counsel, and as well as crisis intervention services

title: Exhibition of contemporary Emarati art gets under way in Kochi
content: Month-long exhibition is being organised by Abu Dhabi-based Rizq Art Initiative in association with the Kerala Lalithakala Akademi, Department of Cultural Affairs, and Abu Dhabi Art

title: Documentary showcasing legendary shadow puppetry artiste to expand reach of art form
content: Nizhal Yathrikan, based on Ramachandra Pulavar’s life and work, directed by theatre artist Saheer Ali premieres in Kochi

title: Hyderabad’s Write Club brings together writers of all ages and genres
content: Write Club founded by Sravanthi Talluri has published four anthologies and held nearly 600 meet-ups in the last nine years

title: Sahitya Akademi-winning author Ramesh Nayak is the voice of the marginalised
content: The writer from Telangana is the first tribal person and the youngest recipient to get the Telugu literature award

title: ‘Why is he hiding behind the curtain?’, a graphic novelette for children, takes readers into the world of Kerala’s performing arts
content: Written by Arun Narayan and illustrated by Sanusha US, Why is he hiding behind the curtain? is a delightful introduction to Kathakali and Ottanthullal

title: Senior Yakshagana bhagavatha Subrahmanya Dhareshwara passes away
content: A Karnataka Rajyotsava awardee, Dhareshwara was a professional ‘bhagavatha’ in Yakshagana melas like Amrutheswari, Hiremahalingeshwara, Panchalinga and Perdoor

title: Id prayers disallowed at Srinagar's Jama Masjid for eighth year in a row
content: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq said it is very unfortunate that children in Kashmir are growing up without witnessing the spiritually uplifting Id prayers at the Eidgah

title: Naidu orders ‘Farm to Home’ model to link Andhra Pradesh farmers directly to consumers
content: Reviewing agriculture and allied sectors, the Chief Minister directs region-wise crop plans, MSP despite adverse weather, and plans to develop Rayalaseema as a horticulture hub under the Purvodaya scheme

title: ED raids former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s houses in CMRL pay-off case
content: ED searches unfolded at 12 locations across Kerala, including residence of former Minister and T. Veena’s husband, P.A. Mohamed Riyas, in Kozhikode, and houses of mining company’s promoters, among other localities

title: CM Vijay’s signature suit: Has Tamil Nadu’s new Chief Minister sparked a blazer trend?
content: From T Nagar clothing stores to custom tailoring studios, we explore whether Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay’s black-and-white suit has inspired a new fashion trend in Tamil Nadu, and how much it costs to recreate his look

title: Exalogic-CMRL pay-off case: ED conducts simultaneous raids in Kerala’s Ernakulam
content: In Ernakulam, searches are being conducted at the corporate office of CMRL in Aluva, the residence of its managing director Sasidharan Kartha, also in Aluva, and the office of a sister concern of CMRL

title: Idli, Chutney And A Leadership Crisis: Karnataka's Breakfast Politics
content: The fact that a breakfast meeting between the two top leaders of the same party has to be publicly announced does evoke curiosity.

title: US-Iran War LIVE: Iran Calls US Strikes 'Bad Faith', Warns Of Consequences As Talks Continue
content: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

title: Exclusive: World's Newest Country Is Without Medicine. And It's Getting Worse
content: For the people of South Sudan, the destruction of an MSF-run hospital meant the disappearance of the only secondary healthcare facility serving nearly 250,000 people across the region.

title: The China Factor In The New India-US Critical Minerals Deal
content: The United States and other nations depend heavily on China for these minerals. But in recent years, especially under President Donald Trump's administration, Washington has pushed to diversify its sourcing to reduce reliance on Beijing.

title: Supreme Court Special Intensive Revision LIVE Updates: Poll Body Can Conduct Exercise, Rules Top Court
content: Supreme Court Special Intensive Revision Hearing LIVE Updates: The petitions challenge the legality of the SIR exercise undertaken by the poll body.

title: NASA Rolls Out Moon Base Plan, Signs Deals For Landers, Rovers, Drones
content: The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies.

title: Trump Team Proposes Non-Disclosure Agreements For Staff To Stop Media Leaks
content: A proposed notice is expected to be officially published seeking comment on a draft NDA to be used by federal agencies for "both new and existing employees."

title: After Bengaluru Scare, Nagpur On Ebola Alert, Uganda Returnee In 21-Day Isolation
content: The current outbreak involves the Bundibugyo strain of the Ebola virus, which spreads through close contact with infected bodily fluids such as blood, sweat, vomit or faeces.

title: Rise In Suicides Among Immigration Detainees Since Trump Took Office: Report
content: An AP probe found that at least 10 detainees, all men, have died by suicide since President Donald Trump took office in January 2025, a pace that exceeds growth in detainee population

title: 8th Pay Commission: 400% Salary Hike, 5 Fitment Factor Formula, And All You Need To Know
content: 8th Pay Commission: Instead of asking for one fitment factor for everyone, the association has proposed five fitment factors for different pay levels.

title: Bengal's Hakimpur Border Sees Rise In Movement After Crackdown On Immigrants
content: At least 100 to 200 people have gathered at the Hakimpur checkpost since Monday, an official told NDTV.

title: Video: Teen's Father In Pune Porsche Case Celebrates Bail With Family
content: Vishal Agarwal, father of the minor alleged to have been driving the Porsche car under the influence of alcohol in May 2024 in Pune that killed two engineers, was granted bail on March 10.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Quad Is Slowly Going Quiet - But For A Reason
content: The Quad is gradually moving away from high-profile summitry toward a quieter, more embedded form of strategic coordination.

title: "Election Commission Has Power": What Supreme Court Said On SIR Exercise
content: The Supreme Court delivered a crucial verdict on Wednesday on a series of petitions challenging the Election Commission of India's (ECI) move to carry out a Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls.

title: Three reports, one story: MiraOne launches precision medicine platform in Mumbai
content: a comprehensive blood test that promises to transform the way we study our body was launched on Saturday in Mumbai at ImagiNXT conference attended by an impressive audience . MiraOne generates three reports to tell one story from a single draw of blood . gives a complete picture - your blood markers, the DNA you inherited and how you respond to medicines .

title: Which city is India’s costliest office market? Here's how the top cities rank
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. title: Dera chief Ram Rahim walks out on 30-day parole, his 16th since 2020
content: govt granted another 30 days' parole to Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh . he is serving 20 years in prison for raping two women disciples . his release was his 16th since 2020 and his second this year .

title: Hundreds try to flee to Bangladesh as Bengal CM Adhikari says quit India
content: dozens of Bangladeshis without the documents required to stay in India make a bee-line for the border exit in Bengal's North 24 Parganas . the BJP govt's "detect-delete-deport" strategy for illegal immigrants was implemented . a day after the first two "holding centres" for detained or imprisoned illegal immigrants opened in Malda and Murshidabad .

title: ED raids ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s house in CMRL-linked money laundering case
content: ED officials are carrying out raids at 10 locations linked to former chief minister . searches are also underway at the residence of CMRL Managing director Sasidharan Kartha and former minister Mohammed Riyas . the action follows the high court order permitting the ED to continue its investigation .
